text: Today we present a case of endobronchial carcinoid recurrence at the site of surgical stump in a 64-year-old male patient, who underwent right upper lobectomy and sleeve resection four years back.
This 64-year-old male patient was diagnosed to have right upper lobe carcinoid four years back for which he underwent a curative intent right upper lobectomy.
He presents to the clinics with complaints of hemoptysis.
Evaluation showed recurrence at the surgical stump and also extrathoracic metastasis.
